name: net-positive-memory
description: A memory Claude carries between chats, stored as plain markdown notes in a folder you choose.
long_description: Net Positive Memory gives Claude a persistent, token-aware memory backed by plain markdown files. Every memory is an ordinary note with a one-line summary and simple tags; links between notes ([[wikilinks]]) form a knowledge graph Claude can walk. Because the notes are just files in a folder you pick, you own them outright: read or edit them in any text editor or in Obsidian, sync them across devices, or delete them at any time. Nothing leaves your machine. Claude searches and reads only the few notes it needs rather than loading everything, so memory stays cheap as it grows.

memory_path.title: Memory folder
memory_path.description: Where your notes are stored. Pick an empty folder, or make a new one; Claude will set it up the first time. Tip: a folder inside a synced drive (iCloud, OneDrive, Dropbox) keeps your memory on every device.

live_watch.title: Watch the folder for outside edits
live_watch.description: Update instantly when you change notes in another app such as Obsidian. Leaving this off is fine; Claude re-reads on every use anyway.

tools.name: vault_patch
tools.description: Replace one section or field in place.

tools.name: vault_search
tools.description: Find notes by fields and text.

tools.name: graph_path
tools.description: Shortest link path between two notes.

tools.name: graph_subgraph
tools.description: The cluster around a note or tag.

tools.name: graph_backlinks
tools.description: Notes that link to a note.

tools.name: vault_reindex
tools.description: Rebuild the index after bulk edits.
